description: Narrative cue library for Lori follow-up selection. Listener aid only; never a truth source.

cue_types.label: Parent character through action, body, silence, work, or gesture
safe_followups: What do you remember those hands doing?
Where would you usually see that happen?
What did that gesture mean in your family?
forbidden_moves: Do not infer affection, coldness, trauma, or diagnosis unless the narrator names it.
Do not turn a gesture into a protected fact.
Do not write parent fields directly.
Cue library is listener aid only; it must never write truth directly.

cue_types.label: Grandparent or elder as keeper of memory, craft, rule, or story
safe_followups: Where was that kept?
Who taught you what it meant?
What do you remember watching them do?
forbidden_moves: Do not infer legal authority, religion, or family role beyond what was said.
Do not promote elder details directly.
Cue library is listener aid only; it must never write truth directly.

cue_types.label: Migration, arrival, move, first strange place, first home
safe_followups: What was the first thing that told you this place was different?
What did you notice first when you arrived?
Who was with you at that moment?
forbidden_moves: Do not assume immigration status.
Do not convert arrival scene into placeOfBirth.
Do not overwrite residence fields.
Cue library is listener aid only; it must never write truth directly.

cue_types.label: House, kitchen, porch, yard, neighborhood, room, shelter
safe_followups: Where in the house did that usually happen?
Who else would have been there?
What did that place feel like to you as a child?
forbidden_moves: Do not treat a remembered home as place of birth.
Do not invent address or residence period.
Cue library is listener aid only; it must never write truth directly.

cue_types.label: Labor, duty, money, farm, factory, railroad, domestic work
safe_followups: What sound do you remember from that work?
What did a hard day of that work look like?
Who depended on that work?
forbidden_moves: Do not decide whether this is narrator work or parent work without subject evidence.
Do not infer class judgment or politics.
Cue library is listener aid only; it must never write truth directly.

cue_types.label: Food, recipes, kitchen ritual, table memory
safe_followups: Who usually made that?
Was that everyday food, or did it belong to a special day?
What smell comes back first when you remember it?
forbidden_moves: Do not infer ethnicity, religion, poverty, or health from food.
Do not turn recipe language into a confirmed family tradition without review.
Cue library is listener aid only; it must never write truth directly.

cue_types.label: Keepsake, tool, document, photo, Bible, jar, ticket, shawl
safe_followups: Where was it kept?
Who was allowed to touch it?
What did that object mean in the family?
forbidden_moves: Do not infer ownership or legal identity beyond stated words.
Do not write identity fields from object mentions.
Cue library is listener aid only; it must never write truth directly.

cue_types.label: Accent, changed name, old language, nickname, shame/pride
safe_followups: How did that name sound inside the family?
Who used the old name?
What did it feel like when people said it differently?
forbidden_moves: Do not correct names.
Do not infer legal name change unless explicitly stated.
Do not write protected identity.
Cue library is listener aid only; it must never write truth directly.

cue_types.label: Belonging, assimilation, in-between identity, old/new world
safe_followups: When did you first feel that split?
Who in the family felt it most strongly?
What helped you feel at home?
forbidden_moves: Do not assign ethnicity, nationality, religion, or politics without direct narrator statement.
Cue library is listener aid only; it must never write truth directly.

cue_types.label: Poverty, hunger, danger, discrimination, weather, illness, social pressure
safe_followups: Would you like to stay with that memory for a moment?
What helped people get through it?
Who was looking out for the family then?
forbidden_moves: Do not press for trauma details.
Do not continue normal interview flow if present distress appears.
Safety overrides this cue.
Cue library is listener aid only; it must never write truth directly.

cue_types.label: Protected custom, coded ritual, silence, private family rule
safe_followups: Was that something people talked about, or something everyone simply understood?
Who seemed to know the meaning of it?
Would you like to stay with that memory, or leave it there for now?
forbidden_moves: Never infer religion, ethnicity, trauma, ancestry, or family secret.
Never name a hidden identity unless the narrator names it first.
Do not press for disclosure.
Cue library is listener aid only; it must never write truth directly.

cue_types.label: Lessons, grandkids, what must be remembered
safe_followups: What do you most want them to understand?
Who do you hope hears that someday?
What would you want written down exactly as you said it?
forbidden_moves: Do not turn wisdom into moral judgment.
Preserve narrator wording when possible.
Cue library is listener aid only; it must never write truth directly.
